data.title: A newly created address withdrew 10 million STRK tokens from Binance, worth $2.5 million.
data.description: According to Arkham's monitoring, about five minutes ago, a newly created address withdrew 10 million STRK from Binance, worth approximately $2.5 million.

data.title: The U.S. goods trade deficit was $85.61 billion in August, while the services trade surplus was $26.06 billion.
data.description: The US goods trade deficit in August was $85.61 billion, while the services trade surplus was $26.06 billion. The overall US trade deficit in August was $59.6 billion, compared to a forecast of $61 billion and a revised previous figure of $78.2 billion (originally reported as -$78.3 billion). US exports rose 0.1% month-over-month in August, compared to a 0.3% increase in July; imports fell 5.1% month-over-month, compared to a 5.9% increase in July. (Jinshi)

data.title: 21Shares officially launched the Solana ETF with an initial asset size of $100 million.
data.description: Eric Balchunas, a senior ETF analyst at Bloomberg, wrote on the X platform that 21Shares officially launched the Solana ETF "TSOL" today, with a fee rate of 21 basis points and an initial asset size of $100 million. The Solana ETF has raised a total of $2 billion so far, with funds flowing in almost every day. Considering the current "extreme panic", this is quite a good result.

data.title: US Senator Brandon Gill increased his holdings of Bitcoin and Bitcoin ETF by approximately $300,000.
data.description: U.S. Representative Brandon Gill, a Republican from Texas, disclosed that he has increased his holdings of Bitcoin and Bitcoin ETFs by up to $300,000 in his portfolio. (Bitcoin News)

BlackRock has been trimming its $BTC and $ETH holdings recently.

data.description:
null
source: https://x.com/lookonchain/status/1991133278414274636

data.title: Abu Dhabi Investment Committee Significantly Increases Bitcoin ETF Holdings in Third Quarter
data.description:
According to Odaily, the Abu Dhabi Investment Committee (ADIC) has significantly increased its holdings in Bitcoin ETFs during the third quarter. The committee has raised its shares in BlackRock's IBIT to nearly 8 million, tripling its holdings from the previous quarter. At the end-of-quarter prices, these shares are valued at approximately $518 million. ADIC has expressed its intention to establish a 'small but long-term allocation,' viewing Bitcoin as a store of value similar to gold, and plans to maintain its holdings in its long-term portfolio.

Meanwhile, Abu Dhabi's sovereign fund, Mubadala, has kept its position unchanged with 8.7 million shares in IBIT.

data.title: BlackRock transferred $1.64 billion worth of Bitcoin and Ethereum to Coinbase in the past three days.
data.description: According to Lookonchain, BlackRock appears to have recently reduced its Bitcoin and Ethereum holdings. In the past three days, BlackRock transferred a total of 12,097 BTC (worth $1.11 billion) and 172,049 ETH (worth $533 million) to Coinbase.

data.title: Saylor says Wall Street has not rattled Bitcoin
data.description:
Saylor says Bitcoin is becoming more stable as its volatility has dropped from 80% to about 50% since 2020.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/saylor-says-wall-street-has-not-rattled-btc/

data.title: Trump may select the next Federal Reserve Chairman before Christmas.
data.description: According to market sources, Trump may select the next Federal Reserve Chairman before Christmas.

data.title: Bitcoin treasury company DDC Enterprise partners with Kraken to enhance trading and custody capabilities.
data.description: DDC Enterprise, a Bitcoin treasury company listed on NYSE America, a subsidiary of the New York Stock Exchange, announced a partnership with Kraken and integration with the exchange's platform. DDC will be able to utilize Kraken's institutional-grade trading and custody solutions to enhance its trading and custody capabilities. The company currently holds 1083 Bitcoins. (Businesswire)

data.title: Abu Dhabi Investment Council (ADIC) held over $500 million in BlackRock IBIT holdings in the third quarter.
data.description: The Abu Dhabi Investment Committee (ADIC) significantly increased its holdings of Bitcoin ETFs in the third quarter, raising its stake in BlackRock IBIT to nearly 8 million units, a three-fold increase from the previous quarter, worth approximately $518 million at the end of the quarter. ADIC stated it is building a "small but long-term allocation" and views Bitcoin as a store of value similar to gold, holding it consistently in its long-term portfolio. Meanwhile, Abu Dhabi's sovereign wealth fund, Mubadala, maintained its holdings of 8.7 million IBIT units. (Bloomberg)

data.title: Bitcoin’s Value Comes From Its Utility, Not Short-Term Price Drops: Bitwise CIO
data.description:
Bitwise CIO Matt Hougan says Bitcoin’s recent drop is “short-term noise” and doesn’t affect its long-term value.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/bitcoins-value-comes-from-its-utility-not-short-term-price-drops-bitwise-cio/

data.title: “Hawk Tuah” Star Sued Over $325K Meme Coin ‘Designed to Crash’ — Will Holders Recover?
data.description:
Haliey “Hawk Tuah” Welch is facing allegations of promoting $HAWK, a memecoin investors claim was engineered to crash, after reportedly being paid $325,000 to hype the token.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/hawk-tuah-hawk-lawsuit/

data.title: Naver's board of directors is expected to make a final confirmation next week regarding the acquisition of Upbit's parent company, Dunamu.
data.description: Sources indicate that Naver, South Korea's largest portal site, is expected to confirm its acquisition plan for Dunamu, the operator of Upbit, South Korea's largest cryptocurrency exchange, at its board meeting next week. Sources familiar with the matter revealed that Dunamu plans to hold a board meeting on November 26 to confirm the specific details of the acquisition. Naver reportedly plans to push through a full stock swap via its fintech subsidiary, Naver Financial, to make Dunamu a wholly owned subsidiary, a move aimed at expanding its business footprint in the digital finance sector. (koreatimes)

data.title: Ave.ai and XPIN have partnered to launch a contract trading competition with a total prize pool of $100,000.
data.description: Ave.ai has officially announced a large-scale contract trading competition in partnership with XPIN. The event will officially begin on November 19th at 21:00 (UTC+8) and last for one week. The competition features a $XPIN prize pool worth a total of $100,000 USD. Reaching the minimum trading volume threshold will earn users a reward equivalent to 40,000 USDT; the top 200 users by trading volume will share a reward equivalent to 55,000 USDT. Both rewards can be claimed simultaneously. Furthermore, during the event, registering with the Ave wallet and trading $XPIN contracts will automatically earn a 1.1x edgeX points bonus. Points rewards will also be available for trading non-participating cryptocurrencies. An Ave.ai spokesperson stated, "This is Ave.ai's first large-scale contract trading competition. Led by XPIN, a high-quality project that has its first token listing on the Ave platform, this event signifies Ave.ai's successful completion of a full price discovery loop from 'token launch -> spot trading -> contract trading.' In the future, Ave will continue to collaborate with edgeX to list more high-quality tokens for contract trading. Simultaneously, we extend an invitation to the industry, welcoming more high-quality projects to partner with Ave to jointly launch decentralized perpetual contracts."

data.title: New BTC wallets under pressure from high cost basis
data.description:
BTC short-term buyers are 95% under water, and are facing challenges to hold. BTC is testing multiple levels of cost basis, with deep liquidity around $82,000, based on general on-chain purchase prices and the cost basis for ETF buyers.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/new-btc-wallets-high-cost-basis-pressure/

data.title: Apex Group plans to acquire investment platform Globacap to advance its tokenization business in the US.
data.description: Apex Group, a financial services provider with over $3 trillion in assets under management, is set to acquire London-based investment platform Globacap, which holds a U.S.-regulated brokerage license, according to two sources familiar with the matter. This acquisition would help Apex advance its regulated fund tokenization project in the U.S., as professional investors' interest in blockchain-based real-world assets (RWAs) continues to grow. In March, UK-based cryptocurrency exchange Archax announced its intention to acquire Globacap's U.S. division. However, according to a recent report citing sources, that deal fell through, and new bidders have emerged. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Standard Chartered firmly believes the Fed will cut rates in December: Non-farm payroll data is likely to be very weak.
data.description: Despite significant divisions among Federal Reserve policymakers ahead of their December meeting, Standard Chartered Bank says this is unlikely to prevent the Fed from continuing to cut rates, warning that weak expectations for the labor market will continue to dominate monetary policy. Steve Englander, Global Head of G10 FX Research and North American Macro Strategy at Standard Chartered, stated in a recent report: “We maintain our view that the FOMC will cut rates in December, primarily because we believe that the employment data for September-November is likely to be very weak, which should be enough to push Fed centrists towards the rate-cutting camp.” (Jinshi)

data.title: Analysis: The sharp drop in Bitcoin triggered a chain reaction, with BlackRock's ETFs experiencing record redemptions.
data.description: Investors withdrew over $500 million from BlackRock's iShares Bitcoin Trust, marking the largest single-day outflow since the fund's inception. Data shows that the exchange-traded fund saw a net outflow of $523 million on Tuesday, marking the fifth consecutive day of net redemptions and a new all-time high. (Jinshi)

data.title: Bitcoin ATMs appear in Nairobi shopping malls after Kenya's first cryptocurrency law came into effect.
data.description: Days after Kenya implemented its first comprehensive cryptocurrency law, ATMs branded "Bankless Bitcoin" appeared in several large shopping malls in Nairobi, offering local residents the service of exchanging cash for cryptocurrency. This follows Kenya's implementation of the Virtual Asset Service Providers Act 2025 on November 4th, the country's first formal licensing framework for cryptocurrency platforms, including wallet operators, exchanges, and custody institutions. (Cointelegraph)
